channel.title: Frontion Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thailand to Put 44 Opposition Figures on Trial Over Royal Insult Law Reform Bid
item.description: The Supreme Court ordered trial for current and former lawmakers from the People's Party and dissolved Move Forward party who attempted to amend the lese-majeste law, facing potential lifetime bans from office.

item.title: ⚽🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Says Iran Can Play at World Cup but Bars Those With IRGC Ties
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed Iranian players can participate in the 2026 World Cup hosted in the US, but individuals with connections to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps will be denied entry.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Rejects Trump Leadership Rift Claims, Presents Unified Front
item.description: Iranian officials including President Pezeshkian and Foreign Minister Araghchi dismissed Trump assertions of internal divisions, emphasizing iron unity and complete obedience to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Unblocks €90 Billion Ukraine Loan, Approves New Russia Sanctions
item.description: The European Union gave final approval to a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine and a 20th round of sanctions on Russia after Hungary and Slovakia dropped objections following Ukraine restoration of Russian oil flows.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Deploys Long-Range Missiles in Southern Shield Buildup Against China
item.description: Japan has deployed long-range missiles to Kyushu as part of a southern defense strategy, citing its most severe security environment since 1945 and growing concerns about US commitment to regional defense.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines Ex-President Duterte to Stand Trial at ICC for Crimes Against Humanity
item.description: The International Criminal Court has confirmed that former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte will stand trial for extrajudicial killings during his war on drugs, which killed thousands.

item.title: 💼 Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs, 10 Percent of Workforce, While Microsoft Offers Buyouts
item.description: Meta announced its largest layoff since 2023, cutting one in ten employees after spending billions on AI development, while Microsoft offers voluntary buyouts to 8,750 workers.

item.title: 🇪🇸🇺🇸 Pentagon floats suspending Spain from NATO over Iran rift
item.description: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies that failed to support US operations in the Iran war, including potentially suspending Spain from the alliance and reviewing US position on Britain's Falkland Islands claim.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚠️ Iran air defenses activated over Tehran amid suspected hostile activity
item.description: Iranian state media reported air defense systems were activated in parts of Tehran following reports of suspected hostile aerial activity, the first such incident since the Iran-US ceasefire began.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump rules out using nuclear weapons against Iran
item.description: President Trump stated he would not use nuclear weapons against Iran, saying 'Why would I use a nuclear weapon when we've decimated them without it?' after previous threats about destroying Iranian civilization.

item.title: 🚢💰 US Navy reviews Ford-class carrier costs amid budget pressures
item.description: The Navy is analyzing costs and design of future Ford-class aircraft carriers USS William J. Clinton and USS George W. Bush to ensure they 'make sense' amid scrutiny of the $13 billion per ship program.

item.title: 🇨🇳🤖 China's DeepSeek releases new AI model with 'drastically reduced' costs
item.description: DeepSeek released a new AI model capable of processing extra-long texts at significantly lower costs, intensifying the US-China AI competition following last year's shockwaves from its reasoning model.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Trump Says King Charles Visit Could Help Repair US-UK Relations
item.description: President Trump told BBC that King Charles upcoming visit to the US could help mend strained relations between the two countries.

item.title: 🇻🇪 US Soldier Charged After Winning ,000 Betting on Maduro Removal
item.description: A US soldier allegedly used classified information to make profitable trades on Polymarket about Venezuelan President Maduro, according to the Justice Department.

item.title: 🇨🇳 White House Claims Chinese Firms Engaged in Mass AI Theft
item.description: A memo from the White House says Chinese companies are wrongfully distilling US AI models, calling it a significant threat to American technological advantage.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Elections Test Hindu Right in Bengali Heartland
item.description: PM Modis BJP has been making gains in West Bengal, but critics decry an audit that removed nine million voters, many of them Muslim.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe Considers Mutual Defense Outside NATO Framework
item.description: EU nations are reassessing their collective defense obligations as President Trump continues to threaten leaving the NATO alliance.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi Splattered With Red Liquid in Berlin
item.description: Iran's exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid after a news briefing in Berlin where he criticized the US-Iran ceasefire. The attack occurred as he left Germany's federal news conference building.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire by 3 Weeks
item.description: President Trump announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire by three weeks after talks at the White House. The meeting between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors was the second in a week, representing the first direct diplomatic talks in decades.

item.title: 🇨🇦🇺🇸 Millions of Americans May Now Qualify for Canadian Citizenship Under New Law
item.description: A recent change to Canada's citizenship requirements has led to a surge in applications from Americans. The new law, which took effect December 15, allows those with Canadian parents or grandparents to claim citizenship more easily.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian drone strike kills married couple, injures 15 in Odesa residential buildings
item.description: A Russian overnight drone attack on multiple residential buildings in Odesa killed two civilians and wounded 15 others, the latest in a series of strikes on southern Ukrainian cities.

item.title: ⚛️ Seven torture chambers holding Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant staff uncovered
item.description: Ukrainian human rights organization Truth Hounds has identified at least seven torture chambers used to detain staff of the occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, revealing systematic abuse of Ukrainian personnel.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US offers $4 million bounty for Chinese money launderer linked to crypto scams
item.description: The US State Department announced a $4 million reward for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, a Chinese national who fled after admitting to laundering funds from cryptocurrency scams targeting victims across Southeast Asia.

item.title: 🚀 Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier groups for anti-drone defense
item.description: The US Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups as the threat of one-way attack drones in the Middle East intensifies following Iranian attacks on shipping.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran war created biggest ever energy security threat, IEA warns
item.description: The International Energy Agency declared the US-Iran conflict has created the single largest threat to global energy security in history, with Strait of Hormuz disruptions sending shockwaves through oil markets worldwide.
